steps.title: Keep the predicates parallel
steps.content: The subject is The lock, and the sentence gives two things the lock would do: it would increase the lakes' salinity and would form a barrier. After the shared modal would, both verbs should be base forms.

steps.title: Match the implied modal
steps.content: Choice A uses a present-tense singular verb, Choice C uses a participle, and Choice D uses a past-tense form. The parallel structure requires would increase and create, with the second would implied. The answer is B.
